verses.text: At the potter's house Jeremiah watches a marred vessel reshaped into another. The clay is reworked. God teaches His sovereignty through the potter's patient hand.
At the potter's house Jeremiah watches a marred vessel reshaped into another. The clay is reworked. God teaches His sovereignty through the potter's patient hand.
At the potter's house Jeremiah watches a marred vessel reshaped into another. The clay is reworked. God teaches His sovereignty through the potter's patient hand.
At the potter's house Jeremiah watches a marred vessel reshaped into another. The clay is reworked. God teaches His sovereignty through the potter's patient hand.
'Cannot I do with you as this potter?' for 'as the clay is in the potter's hand, so are ye in mine hand.' God's sovereignty is absolute. The nation is His to shape, mar, and remake as He wills.
'Cannot I do with you as this potter?' for 'as the clay is in the potter's hand, so are ye in mine hand.' God's sovereignty is absolute. The nation is His to shape, mar, and remake as He wills.
If a doomed nation turns from evil, God relents; if a blessed nation turns to evil, He withholds good. His dealings answer their conduct. Repentance and rebellion both move the hand of the Potter.

If a doomed nation turns from evil, God relents; if a blessed nation turns to evil, He withholds good. His dealings answer their conduct. Repentance and rebellion both move the hand of the Potter.

God pleads, 'Return ye now every one from his evil way'; but they answer, 'There is no hope... we will walk after... our own heart.' Defiant despair. They choose their own devices over the offered mercy.

God pleads, 'Return ye now every one from his evil way'; but they answer, 'There is no hope... we will walk after... our own heart.' Defiant despair. They choose their own devices over the offered mercy.

Such unheard-of folly! His people forgot Him and stumbled from the ancient paths, so He will scatter them before the enemy. Their apostasy is unnatural. To forsake the LORD is stranger than nature ever does.

They plot against Jeremiah, returning evil for good and digging a pit for his soul; he reminds God how he interceded for them. Ingratitude wounds him. The faithful are often repaid with hatred for their love.

They plot against Jeremiah, returning evil for good and digging a pit for his soul; he reminds God how he interceded for them. Ingratitude wounds him. The faithful are often repaid with hatred for their love.

They plot against Jeremiah, returning evil for good and digging a pit for his soul; he reminds God how he interceded for them. Ingratitude wounds him. The faithful are often repaid with hatred for their love.
Stung by their malice, he prays God not to forgive but to deal with them in His anger. He commits judgment to God. Such imprecations leave the unrepentant to the justice they have provoked.
